name: threat-patch-self-analysis
prompt: Can you threat model the skill at skills/.experimental/threat-patch/? It parses Codex security finding CSVs and generates code patches. I'm worried about what happens if someone feeds it a malicious CSV or if the patch workflow has unintended side effects.
expected_output: A threat model identifying CSV parsing risks, source code modification risks, config path handling, and hooks as existing mitigations.
assertions: Output has all 4 sections: Overview, Trust boundaries, Attack surfaces, Criticality calibration
Output identifies CSV input or findings data as potentially untrusted/attacker-controlled
Output notes that the skill modifies source code files as a write/destructive risk
Output references the specific script parse-findings.sh in an attack surface section
Output mentions hooks.json or PreToolUse hooks as an existing mitigation or control
Output includes an explicit out-of-scope or not-applicable section
